content_en:
The greater the anger, the higher the status of the one who controls himself. The Prophet 3 said:

“The strongest man is the one who, when he gets angry and his face reddens and his hackles rise, is able to defeat his anger.” 1

[50] Abu Dawood. [51] Reported by Ahmad, 2/236; the hadith is agreed upon. [52] Ahmad, 5367/, classified as Hasan in sahih Al-Jaami’, 3859.

“Bo not become Angry! Anas -# reported that the Prophet 38 passed by some people

who were wrestling. He asked, “What is this?” They said: “So-andso is the strongest, he can beat anybody.” The Prophet 3s said:

165 (@pBil 1 gD) Ee sal kb ee tee Y

“Shall | not tell you who is even stronger than him? The man who, when he is mistreated by another, controls his anger, has defeated his own shaytaan and the shaytaan of the one who made him angry.” 3

Another way in which we can follow the example of the Prophet # is by making our anger for the sake of Allaah, when His rights are violated. This is the kind of anger which

[53] Reported by Al-Bazzaar, and Ibn Hajir said its Isnaad is sahih. Al-Fath,

[54] Agreed upon. Fath al-Baaree, 10375.

"Golden Advice Series 03

Sai > ate oe 3 eel “Do you seek to intervene concerning one of the punishments

[55] Abu Mas’ud Al-Ansaree # narrated: Once a man said to Allaah's Messenger "0 Allaah's Prophet 3#! | may not attend the (compulsory congregational) prayer because so-and-so (the /maam) prolongs the prayer when he leads us for it. The narrator added: “I never saw the Prophet #3 more furious in giving advice than he was on that day. The Prophet % said:

wl de b ra<io ingle (SEY each lel & sles 35 fas \5 PRAT oe ob 5 s

“O people! Some of you make others dislike good deeds (the prayers).

So whoever leads the people in prayer should shorten it because among

them there are the sick, the weak and the needy (having some jobs to

[56] ‘Aa’ishah (sr wi es) narrated: The Prophet 3 entered upon me while there was a curtain having pictures (of animals) in the house. His face got red with anger, and then he got hold of the curtain and tore it into pieces.

The Prophet # said:

5p ods Shad Soll weal gy cide 01 Lh yy

"Such people as paint these pictures will receive the severest punishment on the Day of Resurrection." (Bukhaaree)

Do 'hot Become Angry! prescribed by Allaah?” '*"!,

when he was asked questions that he disliked**, and so on. His anger was purely for the sake of Allaah.

Knowing that resisting anger is one of the signs of righteousness (taqwa): The righteous (A/-Muttaqoon) are

[57] The Quraish people became very worried about the Makhzumiya lady who had committed theft. They said, “Nobody can speak (in favour of the lady) to Allaah’s Prophet 38 and nobody dares do that except Usama who is the favorite of Allaah's Messenger 38." When Usama spoke to Allaah's Prophet 4 about that matter, Allaah's Messenger 3 said:

“Do you intercede (with me) to violate one of the legal punishments of Allaah?” Then he got up and addressed the people, saying:

Jal gy Sl de (uh Gn es Os hy a 83

BG sed Li5s wink EL Lbs of

“O people! The nations before you went astray because if a noble person committed theft, they used to leave him, but if a weak person among them committed theft, they used to inflict the legal punishment on him. By Allaah, if Fatima, the daughter of Muhammad committed theft, Muhammad will cut off her hand!” (Bukhaaree)

[58] Abu Hurayrah -# reported: Allaah's Messenger # addressed us and said:

“0 people, Allah has made Hajj obligatory for you; so perform Hajj.”

Golden Advice Series 03

those praised by Allaah in the Qur’aan and by His Messenger 48. Paradise as wide as heaven and earth has been prepared for them. One of their characteristics is:

Sally EN GubeaIG FOG Kat a Spas Gi} be KES iG elles

“Those who spend (in Allaah’s Cause) in prosperity and in adversity, [they] repress anger, and [they] pardon men; verily, Allaah loves Al-Muhsinoon (the good-doers).” [Aal

These are the ones whose good character and beautiful attributes and deeds Allaah has mentioned, and whom people

Thereupon a person said: “Messenger of Allah 3, (is it to be performed) every year?” He (the Prophet 48) kept quiet, and he repeated (these words) thrice, whereupon Allaah's Messenger 2 said:

“If | were to say ‘Yes, it would become obligatory (for you to perform it every year) and you would not be able to do it.

“Leave me with what | have left to you, for those who were before you were destroyed because of excessive questioning, and their opposition to their Prophets. So when | command you to do anything, do it as much as it lies in your power and when | forbid you to do anything, then abandon it.” (Muslim)

eamennieain a ‘Bo not become Angry! admire and want to emulate.

Another of their characteristics is that toe pe lene 135% “.,, when they are angry, they forgive.” [Ash-Shooraa (42):47]

Listening to reminders: It is natural to be irritated by something but sincere people will remember Allaah when they are reminded, and they will not overstep the mark. Some examples follow:

Ibn ‘Abbaas # reported that a man sought permission to speak to 'Umar bin Al-Khattaab -, then he said: “O son of Al-Khattaab, you are not giving us much and you are not judging fairly between us.” ‘Umar was so angry that he was about to attack the man, but Al-Hurr bin Qays, who was one of those present, said: “O Ameer Al-Mu’mineen, Allaah 4% said to His Prophet 8:

dened oe Sas ath, 25 wat b>

‘Show forgiveness, enjoin what is good, and turn away from the foolish’ [Al-A’raaf (7):199].

This man is one of the foolish.” By Allaah, ‘Umar could go no further after Al-Hurr had recited this Aayah to him, and he was a man who was careful to adhere to the Book of Allaah.'"!

Knowing the bad effects of anger: The negative effects of anger are many; in short they cause damage to one’s own self and to others. The angry person may utter words of slander and obscenity; he may attack others (physically) in an uncontrolled manner, even to the point of killing. The following story contains

‘Ilqgimah bin Waa’il reported that his father 4 told him: “I was sitting with the Prophet #8 when a man came to him leading another man by a rope. He said, ‘O Messenger of Allaah, this man killed my brother’ The Messenger of Allaah 3 asked him:

“Did you kill him?’

He said, ‘Yes, | killed him.’ He asked,

‘How did you kill him?’

Anger could lead to less than killing, such as wounding and breaking bones. If the one who caused the anger runs away, the angry person turns his anger in on himself, so he may tear his clothes, or strike his cheeks, or have a fit, or fall unconscious, or he may break dishes and plates, or break furniture.

In the worst cases, anger results in social disasters and the breaking of family ties, i.e., divorce. Ask many of those who divorced their wives, and they will tell you: it was in a moment of anger.''! This divorce results in misery for the children, regret

[61] If a man divorces his wife in a state of anger and his anger is such that he does not know what he was saying, his divorce is not valid. The Prophet 3 said:

Do not become Angry!

and frustration, a hard and difficult life, all as a result of anger. If they had remembered Allaah 3%, come to their senses, restrained their anger and sought refuge with Allaah 4s, none of this would have happened. Going against the Sharee‘ah only results in loss.

The damage to health that results from anger can only be described by doctors, such as thrombosis, high blood pressure, tachycardia (abnormally rapid heartbeat) and hyperventilation (rapid, shallow breathing), which can lead to fatal heart attacks, diabetes, etc. We ask Allaah 4 for good health.

The angry person should think about himself during moments of anger: If the angry person could see himself in the mirror when he is angry, he would hate himself and the way he looks. If he could see the way he changes, and the way his body and limbs shake, how his eyes glare and how out of control and crazy his behaviour is, he would despise himself and be revolted by his own appearance.

DE 3 Ske Vy SIE Y “No divorce and freeing of slave during annoyance.” (Authenticated by

However, if the angry man still retained his reason while pronouncing the divorce, his divorce is sustained based upon the statement of the Prophet # that:

E15 GOB CE sd Giljag Ae Gy Sot

“There are three things which, whether undertaken seriously or in jest, are treated as serious: marriage, divorce and taking back a wife after a divorce which is not final.” (Abu Dawood)

"Golden Advice Series 03

It is well-known that inner ugliness is even worse than outer ugliness; how happy the Shaytaan must be when a person is in this state! We seek refuge with Allaah from the Shaytaan and from failure.
content_id:
Semakin besar kemarahan, semakin tinggi kedudukan orang yang mampu mengendalikan dirinya. Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Orang yang paling kuat adalah orang yang ketika marah, wajahnya memerah dan bulu kuduknya berdiri, namun ia mampu mengalahkan kemarahannya." 1

[50] Abu Dawood. [51] Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, 2/236; hadith ini muttafaq 'alayh. [52] Ahmad, 5367/, diklasifikasikan sebagai Hasan dalam Sahih Al-Jaami', 3859.

"Jangan Marah! Anas meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ melewati beberapa orang

yang sedang bergulat. Beliau bertanya, "Apa ini?" Mereka menjawab: "Si fulan adalah yang paling kuat, dia bisa mengalahkan siapa saja." Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Maukah kalian aku beritahu siapa yang lebih kuat darinya? Yaitu seseorang yang ketika dizalimi oleh orang lain, ia mengendalikan kemarahannya, sehingga ia telah mengalahkan syaitannya sendiri dan syaitan orang yang membuatnya marah." 3

Cara lain untuk meneladani Nabi ﷺ adalah dengan menjadikan kemarahan kita karena Allah, ketika hak-hak-Nya dilanggar. Inilah jenis kemarahan yang

[53] Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bazzaar, dan Ibn Hajar menyatakan isnad-nya sahih. Al-Fath,

[54] Muttafaq 'alayh. Fath al-Baaree, 10375.

"Seri Nasihat Emas 03

"Apakah engkau berusaha campur tangan mengenai salah satu hukuman

[55] Abu Mas'ud Al-Anshari meriwayatkan: Suatu ketika seorang laki-laki berkata kepada Rasulullah ﷺ "Wahai Nabi Allah ﷺ! Saya mungkin tidak bisa menghadiri salah (berjamaah wajib) karena si fulan (imam) memperpanjang salah ketika mengimami kami." Perawi menambahkan: "Saya tidak pernah melihat Nabi ﷺ lebih marah dalam memberikan nasihat daripada hari itu. Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Wahai manusia! Sebagian dari kalian membuat orang lain tidak menyukai amal kebaikan (salah).

Maka barangsiapa yang mengimami orang-orang dalam salah, hendaklah ia mempersingkatnya karena di antara

mereka ada yang sakit, yang lemah, dan yang mempunyai keperluan (pekerjaan yang harus

[56] 'Aa'isyah meriwayatkan: Nabi ﷺ masuk menemuiku sementara di rumah ada tirai yang bergambar (binatang). Wajah beliau memerah karena marah, lalu beliau mengambil tirai itu dan merobeknya menjadi potongan-potongan.

Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Orang-orang yang melukis gambar-gambar ini akan menerima siksa yang paling berat pada Hari Kiamat." (Bukhari)

Jangan Marah! yang ditetapkan oleh Allah?" '*"!,

ketika beliau ditanya pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang tidak beliau sukai**, dan seterusnya. Kemarahan beliau murni karena Allah.

Mengetahui bahwa menahan amarah adalah salah satu tanda ketakwaan: Orang-orang yang bertakwa (Al-Muttaqun) adalah

[57] Orang-orang Quraisy sangat khawatir tentang seorang wanita Makhzumiyyah yang melakukan pencurian. Mereka berkata, "Tidak ada yang bisa berbicara (membela wanita itu) kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan tidak ada yang berani melakukannya kecuali Usamah yang merupakan kesayangan Rasulullah ﷺ." Ketika Usamah berbicara kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang masalah itu, Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda:

"Apakah engkau meminta syafaat (kepadaku) untuk melanggar salah satu hukuman Allah?" Kemudian beliau berdiri dan berkhutbah kepada orang-orang, bersabda:

"Wahai manusia! Umat-umat sebelum kalian tersesat karena jika orang bangsawan mencuri, mereka membiarkannya, tetapi jika orang lemah di antara mereka mencuri, mereka menegakkan hukuman atasnya. Demi Allah, seandainya Fatimah putri Muhammad mencuri, niscaya Muhammad akan memotong tangannya!" (Bukhari)

[58] Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah kepada kami dan bersabda:

"Wahai manusia, Allah telah mewajibkan hajj atas kalian; maka laksanakanlah hajj."

Seri Nasihat Emas 03

mereka yang dipuji oleh Allah dalam Al-Quran dan oleh Rasul-Nya ﷺ. Surga seluas langit dan bumi telah disediakan bagi mereka. Salah satu sifat mereka adalah:

"(Yaitu) orang-orang yang menafkahkan (hartanya di jalan Allah), baik di waktu lapang maupun sempit, dan orang-orang yang menahan amarahnya dan memaafkan (kesalahan) orang. Allah menyukai orang-orang yang berbuat ihsan." [Ali

Mereka inilah orang-orang yang akhlak mulia, sifat-sifat indah, dan amal perbuatannya telah disebutkan oleh Allah, dan yang dikagumi oleh manusia

Kemudian seseorang bertanya: "Wahai Rasulullah ﷺ, (apakah harus dilaksanakan) setiap tahun?" Beliau ﷺ diam, dan orang itu mengulanginya tiga kali, lalu Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda:

"Seandainya aku katakan 'Ya', niscaya ia menjadi wajib (atas kalian untuk melaksanakannya setiap tahun) dan kalian tidak akan mampu melakukannya.

"Biarkanlah aku dengan apa yang telah aku tinggalkan kepada kalian, karena sesungguhnya orang-orang sebelum kalian binasa karena banyak bertanya dan menentang para Nabi mereka. Maka jika aku memerintahkan kalian untuk melakukan sesuatu, lakukanlah semampu kalian, dan jika aku melarang kalian dari sesuatu, maka tinggalkanlah." (Muslim)

Jangan Marah! dan ingin meneladaninya.

Sifat lain dari mereka adalah bahwa ".,, ketika mereka marah, mereka memberi maaf." [Asy-Syura (42):47]

Mendengarkan nasihat: Adalah wajar untuk merasa kesal terhadap sesuatu, tetapi orang-orang yang tulus akan mengingat Allah ketika mereka diingatkan, dan mereka tidak akan melampaui batas. Berikut beberapa contohnya:

Ibn 'Abbas meriwayatkan bahwa seorang laki-laki meminta izin untuk berbicara kepada 'Umar bin Al-Khattab, lalu ia berkata: "Wahai putra Al-Khattab, engkau tidak banyak memberi kepada kami dan engkau tidak menghakimi dengan adil di antara kami." 'Umar begitu marah sehingga hampir menyerang orang itu, tetapi Al-Hurr bin Qays, yang merupakan salah seorang yang hadir, berkata: "Wahai Amirul Mukminin, Allah ﷻ berfirman kepada Nabi-Nya ﷺ:

'Jadilah engkau pemaaf dan suruhlah orang mengerjakan yang makruf, serta berpalinglah dari orang-orang yang bodoh' [Al-A'raf (7):199].

Orang ini termasuk orang-orang yang bodoh." Demi Allah, 'Umar tidak bisa berbuat apa-apa lagi setelah Al-Hurr membacakan ayat ini kepadanya, dan beliau adalah orang yang sangat berhati-hati dalam berpegang teguh pada Kitab Allah.'"!

Mengetahui dampak buruk kemarahan: Dampak negatif kemarahan sangat banyak; singkatnya, kemarahan menyebabkan kerugian pada diri sendiri dan orang lain. Orang yang marah bisa mengucapkan kata-kata fitnah dan keji; ia bisa menyerang orang lain (secara fisik) dengan cara yang tidak terkendali, bahkan sampai membunuh. Kisah berikut ini mengandung

'Ilqimah bin Waa'il melaporkan bahwa ayahnya menceritakan kepadanya: "Aku sedang duduk bersama Nabi ﷺ ketika datang seorang laki-laki menuntun laki-laki lain dengan tali. Ia berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, orang ini telah membunuh saudaraku.' Rasulullah ﷺ bertanya kepadanya:

"Apakah engkau membunuhnya?'

Ia menjawab, 'Ya, aku membunuhnya.' Beliau bertanya,

'Bagaimana engkau membunuhnya?'

Kemarahan bisa mengakibatkan hal yang kurang dari pembunuhan, seperti melukai dan mematahkan tulang. Jika orang yang menyebabkan kemarahan melarikan diri, orang yang marah akan mengarahkan kemarahannya pada dirinya sendiri, sehingga ia mungkin merobek pakaiannya, atau memukul pipinya, atau mengalami kejang, atau pingsan, atau ia mungkin memecahkan piring dan gelas, atau merusak perabotan.

Dalam kasus terburuk, kemarahan mengakibatkan bencana sosial dan putusnya tali kekeluargaan, yaitu perceraian. Tanyakan kepada banyak orang yang menceraikan istri mereka, dan mereka akan memberitahumu: itu terjadi di saat marah.''! Perceraian ini mengakibatkan kesengsaraan bagi anak-anak, penyesalan

[61] Jika seorang laki-laki menceraikan istrinya dalam keadaan marah dan kemarahannya sedemikian rupa sehingga ia tidak menyadari apa yang dikatakannya, maka perceraiannya tidak sah. Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

Jangan Marah!

dan frustrasi, kehidupan yang keras dan sulit, semuanya akibat kemarahan. Seandainya mereka mengingat Allah ﷻ, sadar, menahan amarah mereka dan berlindung kepada Allah ﷻ, semua ini tidak akan terjadi. Menentang syariat hanya menghasilkan kerugian.

Kerusakan kesehatan yang diakibatkan oleh kemarahan hanya bisa dijelaskan oleh para dokter, seperti trombosis, tekanan darah tinggi, takikardia (detak jantung yang cepat secara abnormal) dan hiperventilasi (pernapasan yang cepat dan dangkal), yang dapat menyebabkan serangan jantung fatal, diabetes, dan sebagainya. Kita memohon kepada Allah ﷻ kesehatan yang baik.

Orang yang marah hendaknya memikirkan dirinya sendiri di saat-saat kemarahan: Jika orang yang marah bisa melihat dirinya di cermin ketika ia marah, ia pasti akan membenci dirinya sendiri dan penampilannya. Jika ia bisa melihat cara ia berubah, dan cara tubuh serta anggota badannya gemetar, bagaimana matanya melotot dan betapa tidak terkendali serta gilanya perilakunya, ia pasti akan menghina dirinya sendiri dan jijik dengan penampilannya sendiri.

"Tidak ada perceraian dan pembebasan budak saat dalam keadaan marah." (Disahihkan oleh

Namun, jika orang yang marah itu masih memiliki akalnya saat mengucapkan talak, maka perceraiannya tetap berlaku berdasarkan sabda Nabi ﷺ bahwa:

"Ada tiga perkara yang baik dilakukan dengan sungguh-sungguh maupun main-main, tetap dianggap serius: pernikahan, perceraian, dan rujuk setelah talak yang belum final." (Abu Dawood)

"Seri Nasihat Emas 03

Sudah diketahui bahwa keburukan batin bahkan lebih parah daripada keburukan lahir; betapa bahagianya syaitan ketika seseorang berada dalam keadaan seperti ini! Kita berlindung kepada Allah dari syaitan dan dari kegagalan.

Knowing the bad effects of الغضب: The negative effects of الغضب are many; in short they cause damage to one’s own self and to others. The angry person may utter words of slander and obscenity; he may attack others (physically) in an uncontrolled manner, even to the point of killing. The following story contains ‘Ilqgimah bin Waa’il reported that his الأب 4 told him: “I was sitting with النبي ﷺ #8 عندما a man came to him leading another man by a rope. قال, ‘O Messenger of Allaah, this man killed my الأخ’ الرسول ﷺ of Allaah 3 asked him: “Did you kill him?’ قال, ‘Yes, | killed him.’ He asked, ‘How did you kill him?’ الغضب could lead to less than killing, مثل wounding and breaking bones. إذا the one who caused the الغضب runs away, the angry person turns his الغضب in on himself, لذا he may tear his clothes, or strike his cheeks, or have a fit, or fall unconscious, or he may break dishes and plates, or break furniture. In the worst cases, الغضب results in social disasters and the breaking of الأسرة ties, i.e., divorce. Ask many of those who divorced their wives, and they will tell you: it was in a moment of الغضب.''! This divorce results in misery for the الأولاد, regret [61] إذا a man divorces his الزوجة in a state of الغضب and his الغضب is such that he does not know what he was saying, his divorce is not valid. النبي ﷺ 3 said: Do not become Angry! and frustration, a hard and difficult الحياة, all ونتيجة لذلك of الغضب. إذا they had remembered Allaah 3%, come to their senses, restrained their الغضب and sought refuge with Allaah 4s, none of this would have happened. Going against the Sharee‘ah only results in loss. The damage to الصحة that results from الغضب can only be described by doctors, مثل thrombosis, high blood pressure, tachycardia (abnormally rapid heartbeat) and hyperventilation (rapid, shallow breathing), which can lead to fatal القلب attacks, diabetes, etc. We ask Allaah 4 for الخير الصحة. The angry person should think about himself خلال moments of الغضب: إذا the angry person could see himself in the mirror عندما he is angry, he would الكراهية himself and the الطريق he looks. إذا he could see the الطريق he changes, and the الطريق his الجسد and limbs shake, how his eyes glare and how out of control and crazy his السلوك is, he would despise himself and be revolted by his own appearance. DE 3 Ske Vy SIE Y “No divorce and freeing of slave خلال annoyance.” (Authenticated by غير أن, إذا the angry man still retained his العقل بينما pronouncing the divorce, his divorce is sustained based upon the statement of النبي ﷺ # that: E15 GOB CE sd Giljag Ae Gy Sot “There are three things which, whether undertaken seriously or in jest, are treated as serious: marriage, divorce and taking back a الزوجة بعد a divorce which is not final.” (أبو داود) "Golden Advice Series 03 من المعروف that inner ugliness is even worse than outer ugliness; how happy the Shaytaan must be عندما a person is in this state! We seek refuge with Allaah from the Shaytaan and from الخسارة.
